mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive rates longer than markets price in, causing multiple compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative during identified tightening episodes, or the inflation-adjusted policy rate spread shows no predictive power for weekly equity moves.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over the subsequent month regardless of valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns, or the rolling correlation coefficient remains below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 additions for three consecutive months while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls below 4.0 percent, it signals an overheating labor market that triggers wage-price spiral fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor market signal fails to precede equity drawdowns in more than 40% of historical instances, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a divergence where industrial production rebounds faster than housing starts indicates supply chain normalization outpacing demand recovery, which compresses input cost inflation and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The production-housing divergence fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding business hiring cycles that precede earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week through improved corporate cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth shows no predictive relationship with weekly equity returns, or the rolling Spearman rank correlation remains below 0.05 over a 3-year window.
